title: Anti-Gravity Advanced Workflow Possibilities
audience: LinkedIn builders, AI engineers, and technical founders

slides.headline: Anti-Gravity in 2026: Beyond Basic Usage
slides.subtitle: Advanced workflows teams can actually run
slides.accentText: MCP connectors | Multi-agent execution | Extension stack

slides.headline: What changed recently?
slides.accentText: From assistant UX to agent-first execution
bullets: Agents run across editor, terminal, browser
Artifacts verify work with plans and recordings
MCP Store adds connectors inside the IDE

slides.headline: The feature stack that matters
slides.introText: Leverage comes from **MCP + Artifacts + multi-agent flow**
bullets: MCP servers connect live systems in-IDE
Artifacts create auditable action trails
Manager flow coordinates specialist agents

slides.headline: Workflow 1: Data-aware engineering loop
slides.introText: Connect **AlloyDB, BigQuery, and Looker** via MCP for grounded builds
bullets: Inspect schemas before writing features
Generate, run, and optimize SQL in one flow
Validate metrics against Looker semantics

slides.headline: Workflow 2: Autonomous analytics copilot
slides.introText: Turn one question into a **forecast + driver brief**
bullets: Agent discovers datasets through connectors
Runs forecast and contribution analysis
Builds decision summary with source traces

slides.headline: Workflow 3: End-to-end voice agent factory
slides.introText: Use Anti-Gravity as control plane for **voice delivery**
bullets: Agent 1 scaffolds telephony and webhooks
Agent 2 adds STT, orchestration, tool calls
Agent 3 validates browser and API behavior

slides.headline: Workflow 4: Multi-model extension cockpit
slides.introText: Combine AG orchestration with **Codex or Claude Code extensions** where useful
bullets: Community reports Open VSX extension installs
Use model specialization by task
Keep AG as mission control and review hub

slides.headline: Workflow 5: MCP reliability hardening
slides.introText: Treat MCP as infrastructure when connector count grows
bullets: Use gateway patterns to reduce reconnect churn
Enforce explicit env vars and path configs
Monitor tool failures and reconnect loops

slides.headline: How to reduce intimidation in week one
slides.introText: Adopt in phases so teams move with confidence
bullets: Phase 1: single-agent tasks plus reviews
Phase 2: add one MCP connector to delivery
Phase 3: split agents by role and domain

slides.headline: Ready to operationalize Anti-Gravity?
slides.ctaText: Comment AG for the workflow map

caption:
Most people talk about Anti-Gravity like it is just another AI coding assistant.

That is the basic layer.

The bigger play is using it as an execution system where agents can plan, build, verify, and connect to real systems through MCP.

In this carousel, I broke down advanced workflow possibilities I am seeing teams explore:
- Data-aware engineering loops
- Autonomous analytics copilots
- End-to-end voice agent factories
- Multi-model extension cockpit setups
- MCP reliability hardening

If you are overwhelmed, start simple and adopt in phases.

Comment AG if you want the exact rollout template.
